expectations.text: Every research question in the plan is answered one-to-one: S1.RQ1, S1.RQ2, S2.RQ1, S2.RQ2, S3.RQ1, and S3.RQ2 each have a corresponding finding, with none left unanswered
expectations.evidence: Found all labels: S1.RQ1, S1.RQ2, S2.RQ1, S2.RQ2, S3.RQ1, S3.RQ2.

expectations.text: S1.RQ1 is answered EXISTS, naming requireAuth in the auth middleware (src/middleware/auth.ts) with a file:line location and a note on how to reuse it
expectations.evidence: S1.RQ1 names requireAuth with auth middleware file:line evidence and describes reuse for authenticated requests.

expectations.text: S1.RQ2 is answered EXISTS, naming getUserById in the user service (src/users/service.ts) with a file:line location and a note that it returns the full user record and can be reused directly
expectations.evidence: S1.RQ2 names getUserById with user service file:line evidence and says it fetches the full user record for reuse.

expectations.text: The reuse constraint that getUserById assumes the caller is already authenticated is recorded as a caveat on that EXISTS finding
expectations.evidence: The output records that getUserById relies on prior authentication / does no auth check itself.

expectations.text: S2.RQ1 is answered MISSING (no lookup-by-username exists) and names a concrete extension point — where to add the username lookup (e.g. alongside getUserById in src/users/service.ts)
expectations.evidence: S2.RQ1 is classified as missing, distinguishes getUserById as id-based, and points to the user service as the extension point.

expectations.text: S2.RQ2 is answered MISSING (no public/unauthenticated profile route exists) and names a concrete extension point — where to add a new public route fitting the existing routing structure (e.g. a profile route alongside src/routes/account.ts)
expectations.evidence: S2.RQ2 is classified as missing, cites the existing account route structure, and names a route extension point for a public profile route.

expectations.text: S3 raises a BLOCKING finding: the design requires a synchronous order-confirmation send returning an inline delivery result, but the codebase's notifier is asynchronous/queue-only (queueOrderConfirmation) and the order schema has no email field, so the plan's assumption is contradicted
expectations.evidence: S3 has a blocking/blocked finding explaining both the queue-only notifier and missing order email contradiction, with re-planning called out.

expectations.text: S3's status is set to blocked, and the BLOCKING finding explains exactly what is wrong rather than inventing a workaround
expectations.evidence: S3 status set to blocked.

notes: with_skill reached 100.0% pass-rate across all three evals, improving from 94.4% in iteration 1 and widening the baseline delta from +0.11 to +0.22.
The S2-only miss from iteration 1 was fixed: the revised skill output explicitly rejected getUserById as id-based, not username-based, and pointed to a username lookup extension point.
The clarified MISSING-vs-BLOCKING rule held: ordinary missing username/route work stayed researched/MISSING, while the synchronous notification and missing order email contradiction stayed blocked.
with_skill became slightly faster than without_skill on average in iteration 2 (-2.9s) with roughly equal token usage, so the added specificity did not impose a measurable cost in this run.
